description: The weekly pulse on real AI work for managers, small-business owners, and nonprofit leaders.
author: Jason Padgett

editions.label: Countdown 6
editions.title: Eighty Hours of Work. Ninety Minutes.
editions.description: Seven AI agents did what Jason figures was eighty to a hundred hours of work in ninety minutes, plus the setup that keeps a business running when the models change.

editions.label: Episode 002
editions.title: From Org Chart to Operating System
editions.description: Nine days ago I held a constitutional convention with my AI agents. This is what got built, what is honestly still a demo, and why the gates are the part worth copying.

editions.title: Your AI Agents Need an Org Chart
editions.description: One AI agent feels like magic. Three AI agents need management, governance, a shared record, and receipts.

editions.title: I Put Two AIs in a Slack Channel
editions.description: What it looks like when a non-coder runs an AI operating system, using one real Saturday of three-agent podcast production as the case.

editions.title: Don't Marry the Model
editions.description: What the overnight shutdown of Anthropic's Fable 5 and Mythos 5 teaches every business that quietly runs on a single AI model.

editions.title: Done Being Victor Frankenstein
editions.description: One real week of turning copy-paste chaos into agentic workflows, no code required.
